steps.title: Recognize the cost equation structure
steps.content:
The equation $f = 3.25x + 8.50y$ represents a total cost formula.

The general form is:
Total = (unit price) × (quantity) + (unit price) × (quantity)
In this context, each coefficient (3.25 and 8.50) represents a unit price, and each variable ($x$ and $y$) represents a quantity.

steps.title: Determine what 8.50 represents
steps.content: Since 8.50 is the coefficient multiplying $y$, and $y$ represents the number of sandwiches, we know that 8.50 is the unit price of a sandwich. In other words, 8.50 is the price, in dollars, of one sandwich. This is choice B.
